de.uka.ilkd.key.macros

Class AbstractPropositionalExpansionMacro

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.macros.AbstractProofMacro
      • de.uka.ilkd.key.macros.StrategyProofMacro
        • de.uka.ilkd.key.macros.AbstractPropositionalExpansionMacro
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    ProofMacro
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    FullPropositionalExpansionMacro, HeapSimplificationMacro, IntegerSimplificationMacro, PropositionalExpansionMacro, PropositionalExpansionWithSimplificationMacro, SelfcompositionStateExpansionMacro, UpdateSimplificationMacro

    public abstract class AbstractPropositionalExpansionMacro
extends StrategyProofMacro
    The Class AbstractPropositionalExpansionMacro applies purely propositional rules. The names of the set of rules to be applied is defined by the abstract method getAdmittedRuleNames(). This is very helpful to perform many "andLeft", "impRight" or even "andRight" steps at a time.

    • Nested Class Summary

      • Nested classes/interfaces inherited from interface de.uka.ilkd.key.macros.ProofMacro

    • Method Detail

      • asSet

        protected static java.util.Set<java.lang.String> asSet(java.lang.String... strings)

      • getCategory

        public java.lang.String getCategory()
        Description copied from interface: ProofMacro
        Gets the category of this macro. Used as name of the menu under which the macro is sorted. Return null if no submenu is to be created.
        Returns:
        a constant string, or null

      • getAdmittedRuleNames

        protected abstract java.util.Set<java.lang.String> getAdmittedRuleNames()
        Gets the set of admitted rule names.
        Returns:
        a constant non-null set

      • allowOSS

        protected abstract boolean allowOSS()
        Whether this macro includes One Step Simplification.

      • createStrategy

        protected Strategy createStrategy(Proof proof,
                                  PosInOccurrence posInOcc)
        Specified by:
        createStrategy in class StrategyProofMacro

      • ruleApplicationInContextAllowed

        protected boolean ruleApplicationInContextAllowed(RuleApp ruleApp,
                                                  PosInOccurrence pio,
                                                  Goal goal)
        Checks whether the application of the passed rule is ok in the given context.
        Parameters:
        ruleApp - rule to be applied
        pio - context
        goal - context
        Returns:
        true if rule may be applied
